issue_title: Can a Nested schema serialize values from its parent?
issue_body:
My apologies if this is covered in the docs somewhere and I missed it. Is it possible to have a Nested schema that refers to properties of the parent model object?

Assume you have a model that looks something like this:
```python

class UserModel():
    name = Column(String)
    created = Column(Datetime)
    updated = Column(Datetime)
```
And you want to produce serialized output like:
```python
{'meta': {'created': '2018-08-02T10:53:41',
          'updated': '2018-08-02T10:54:04'},
 'name': 'Some Name'}
```

If you define marshmallow schemas like:

```python

from marshmallow import Schema, fields

class MetaSchema(Schema):
    created = fields.DateTime(dump_only=True)
    updated = fields.DateTime(dump_only=True)

class UserSchema(Schema):
     name = fields.String()
     meta = fields.Nested(MetaSchema())
```

But there's no "meta" attribute on the ORM object, I just want to pull out specific properties. Is there a way to tell the Nested schema that it should look for values on the parent?

A guess a more general way to phrase this question would be: How can Marshmallow handle situations where the relationships between model objects is different that the relationships in the serialized output? Should I just be coercing it into the necessary shape with `@post_dump` and `@pre_load` modifiers?

issue_title: Recipe needed for a serializable object containing a dict of sets containing serializable objects
issue_body:
Does a recipe exist to solve the following problem I'm having--  serialize an object that contains a dict of sets of serializable objects?

I've created a real world example to illustrate what I'm trying to achieve and why.  In this example, people are indexed based on the city of where they live.  Each city has a number of zip codes.  I query for people based on city.

here's my gist:  https://gist.github.com/Dowwie/24de24ab751c4cbdd618#file-gistfile1-txt

I do not know how to define the Schema(s) for the PeopleIndex class. I realize that sets aren't supported and that I'd have to convert/revert to lists-- that's not a problem.  The major challenges I'm dealing with relate to that the cities and people aren't known until run-time.  


In pseudo code, I'm trying to do this:
----------------------------
```python
class PeopleIndex(Schema):
    people_index = Dict(Nested(PeopleSchema, many=True))
```

I would love to be able to have certain fields excluded by default, but included only in certain scenarios, without specifying the full list of fields I want every time. For instance, say I have a schema like this:

    class MySchema(Schema):
        id = fields.Integer()
        name = fields.String()
        expensive_field = fields.Function(lambda obj: obj.some_expensive_method())

I don't want `expensive_field` to be serialized 90% of the time, so I would like to be able to do something like this:

    class MySchema(Schema):
        # ...fields from above...
        class Meta:
            fields = ('id', 'name')

    MySchema(include=('expensive_field',))

Currently, it seems that I have to `exclude` the fields I don't want on every use rather than including them only when I need them. Is there currently a way to achieve this? And if not, would it be possible to add this?

issue_title: Option to save last "pre-mature result" in result field upon interruption
issue_body:
Often when training learning algorithms you interrupt a the experiment as soon as you realize that learning is going nowhere or when the algorithm is converging to a lower score. However since the `result` field of an interrupted experiment is always empty it is difficult to evaluate the performance of these interrupted configurations. 

I think that it would be a useful feature if there would be an intuitive way to save the pre-mature result (that has been obtained so far) in the `result` field such that interrupted experiments can be evaluated more easily.

The [staging](https://github.com/Becksteinlab/GromacsWrapper/tree/develop/staging) subpackage contains some untested and unmaintained (and unused) code to write Gridenengine queueing system submission scripts in Python with staging of input and output files. There are much better and well-maintained solutions for this kind of workflow automation. This code is really of little value today and if anyone *really* wants it (I doubt it) they can go into this project's history.

- [x] remove `staging` from GW
https://travis-ci.org/zalando-stups/piu/builds/144773317

We are currently testing [Kubernetes on AWS](https://github.com/zalando-incubator/kubernetes-on-aws) with CoreOS AMIs. We take the AMI ID from https://coreos.com/dist/aws/aws-stable.json and put it into the Senza definition YAML manually. It would be better to have a Senza component to automatically find and use the latest CoreOS AMI (similar to what the `StupsAutoConfiguration` component does for Taupage).

issue_title: iZ3 - wrong interpolant from Farkas proof
issue_body:
Z3 computes a wrong interpolant for the example below.

I think this is due to the "rounding off" done in order to deal with
integer inequalities, which is performed regardless of whether the input
inequality is over the integer or over the rationals.

I'm also attaching a tentative patch for the issue.

Here's an SMT-LIB2 example showing the problem:
-----------------------------------------------

```
(set-logic QF_LRA)

(declare-fun x () Real)
(declare-fun y () Real)
(declare-fun z () Real)

(define-fun A () Bool
  (and
   (not (<= x 0.0))
   (>= (+ (* 10.0 y) (* (- 9.0) x)) 0.0)
   )
  )

(define-fun B () Bool
  (and (>= z 0.0)
       (<= (+ (* 10.0 y) (* 9.0 z)) 0.0))
  )

(compute-interpolant (and (interp A) B))
(exit)

;; computed wrong interpolant:
(define-fun I () Bool
  (>= y (/ 9.0 10.0)))
;; a correct interpolant is: (not (<= y 0.0))

(assert (or (and A (not I)) (and B I)))
(check-sat)
```

Here's a tentative patch:
-------------------------

```
diff --git a/src/interp/iz3proof_itp.cpp b/src/interp/iz3proof_itp.cpp
--- a/src/interp/iz3proof_itp.cpp
+++ b/src/interp/iz3proof_itp.cpp
@@ -2590,6 +2590,9 @@
         if(pstrict && qstrict && round_off)
             Qrhs = make(Sub,Qrhs,make_int(rational(1)));
 #else
+        if (round_off && get_type(Qrhs) != int_type()) {
+            round_off = false;
+        }
         bool pstrict = op(P) == Lt;
         if(qstrict && round_off && (pstrict || !(c == make_int(rational(1))))){
             Qrhs = make(Sub,Qrhs,make_int(rational(1)));
```

issue_title: about the maven dependence，can not run normal

issue_body:
Github doesn't allow me to re-open #70, but this is in the same vein.

Based on this definition from the "spec":

```
remε,σ : RM × Fε,σ × Fε,σ → Fε,σ
remε,σ(rm, f, g) = f                            if  g ∈ FIε,σ
                   NaN                          if  f ∈ FIε,σ ∪ {NaN}
                   NaN                          if  g ∈ FZε,σ ∪ {NaN}
                   rnd(v, rm, isNeg(f), x)      if  x = v(f) − (v(g) ∗ y),
                                                    y = rnd(in, rm, 0, v(f)/v(g))
```

I do believe `fp.rem` should simply return the first argument if the second argument is an infinity.

Currently Z3 is returning `NaN` when the first argument is `+oo`; instead of the argument itself. That is, `fp.rem +oo +oo` should evaluate to `+oo`, but Z3 is currently producing `NaN`.

Same issue with `fp.rem +oo -oo`; `fp.rem -oo +oo`; and `fp.rem -oo -oo`.

I'm trying to debug https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/issues/387.  To do so, I figured my only hope was to use a source level debugger to jump around the code.  I'm on a Mac, so Visual Studio is out.  I like CLion, so I wrote a cmake file to build libz3.dylib.  The library and binary both compile fine, and the C example works.  However, in running the c++ example, it keeps segfaulting here

https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/blob/master/src/sat/sat_solver.cpp#L625

I figured you may doing something clever with the build, since you make an archive file for every directory.  So I ran objdump on every archive file passed to the final linker, and put the object in that order.  Same crash. Off the top of your head, can you think of anything that would cause a segfault there?  I figure I didn't link in the memory manager correctly or something, but the build system is very complex, and I figured I'd just see if there's an obvious problem with what I'm doing.

Thanks.

issue_title: Template - dict which pgen can work with
issue_body:
Currently a template file for  a tool (a project file) is a file, which is read by pgen. If I want to build something on top of pgen, it would be good to have template as a python dictionary. This can be used  for example to change values (we can create an API for tools).

An example - a project needs to set uARM or ARM option for uvision based on a target. It would create a generic template for a project, which is used by pgen. So prior exporting a project, if this target is for uARM, set it in template and export. This can lead to an API per tool to at least provide useful options (methods to cover all will take quite a time).

issue_title: Add check for unicode string value in BVV
issue_body:
I was playing around and ran into some trouble while processing a unicode string at [1]. It's not too hard to handle a unicode string here. Obtaining the correct byte length for multi-byte unicode characters should still work with `len` on unicode strings. It should also return the correct hex encoded representation.

However, I haven't checked elsewhere for similar issues. Would the better solution be to create a hex string in my program before passing it in? Ex: '\xc3\xab\xcc\x81a\xc3\xba' instead of 'ë́aú'

I can submit a pull-request if you'd like the change that handles unicode.

Thank you!

[1] https://github.com/angr/claripy/blob/master/claripy/ast/bv.py#L182
Rather than encode my password in a dotfile, it would be great to use an [API key](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=893195) instead.

issue_title: If-Match:* behaviour is unclear (and crashes) with existing record
issue_body:
On 5.2 it would raise a "400" on existing record.

On current master, it will crash in "500" as it's not handled by `kinto.core.resource` anymore, but it's considered a valid header value by deserialization.

I think we should set the behavior to just ignore it. and process the request anyways. Opinions?

issue_title: Should we remove kinto.core features unused in Kinto ?
issue_body:
`kinto.core` is the toolkit leveraged by Kinto but that can be used to implement domain-specific APIs. It ships a lot of good practices by default.

Historically, `kinto.core` was _Cliquet_ a toolkit in a separate repo.
It has features that were implemented for the _readinglist_, _Syncto_ and _kinto_ projects.

But now it is the core of Kinto, should we keep core features that are unused ?

For example:
- [readonly fields in resources](https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/blob/master/kinto/core/resource/schema.py#L24-L29)
- [resources without permissions (records per user-id)](https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/blob/master/kinto/core/resource/__init__.py#L126)
- [private permission in authz policy](https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/blob/master/kinto/core/authorization.py#L14-L15)
- [newrelic monitoring agent](https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/blob/master/kinto/core/initialization.py#L289-L294)

In other words, should we get rid of these now, and re-introduce them the day we need them, or should we keep them ­— just in case ?

---

Quick poll: https://twitter.com/Natim/status/765884024934002690

issue_title: Endianness of Depth Raw Data from "Save snapshot" Button
issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D415 | 
| Firmware Version                   | 05.09.14.00 | 
| Operating System & Version |  Win 10  |  
| Platform                                 | PC |
| SDK Version                            |  2.13.0                       |  

### Issue Description
Hi, I have an issue with the endianness of depth raw data obtained from the "Save snapshot" button of RealSense Viewer. Reading it as little endian gives the correct depth values for each pixel but doesn't display correctly, and vice versa for big endian. 

Little endian gives the correct depth value (499 mm) but displays the image incorrectly (with colormap jet):
![depth little endian](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22404638/43163697-dc2c90a4-8f5c-11e8-936a-d334814cb13f.png)

Big endian displays the image correctly but gives the wrong depth value (6221 mm >> 499 mm!):
![depth big endian](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22404638/43163696-dc1e369e-8f5c-11e8-9ae2-3b26d6bc52cd.png)

RealSense Viewer snapshot png:
![realsense viewer snapshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/22404638/43163698-dc3a3344-8f5c-11e8-8e6a-3ab55ee5557c.png)

If I read a frame from a bag file recorded in RealSense Viewer, little endian also displays correctly (with the correct depth values), and big endian is the same (displays correctly but wrong depth values). So, this seems to be an issue with the "Save snapshot" button only.

| SDK Version                            |  { 2.14.1 }                          | 
| Language                            |  {C++ with OpenCV }                          | 
| Segment			|                          | 

### Issue Description
I'm having a similar issue to some others where when I plug my camera in once, it works fine, but if I stop the stream and restart it I get a "No Frames Received error" (on the Realsense-Viewer. It just hangs on all the examples / my own script). The only workaround I've figured is to unplug and replug the camera back in, and then it works just fine again... once. It consistently only works once.

I know how to manual reset it using the Realsense-Viewer, but I can't figure out how to code wise. (My workaround currently is to just hardware reset the cameras at the beginning of my script.)

If I try:
> rs2::device dev = device_you_are_using();
> dev.hardware_reset(); //Will disconnect the camera and reconnect it.

I get a "'device_you_are_using' was not declared in scope" error and it fails to compile.

Similarly, if I try: 
> rs2::context ctx;
> rs2::device dev = get_device_from_context(ctx);
> dev.hardware_reset();
> rs2::device_hub hub(ctx);
> dev = hub.wait_for_device(); //Note that device hub will get any device, if you have more than 1 connected it could return the other device

I get a "'get_device_from_context' was not declared in scope" error and it also fails.

Could someone please explain how I can implement this in C++? There is very little / no documentation on hardware_reset(). Thank you!

atomate cannot be run in offline mode with the gzip option of custodian on - custodian will zip up FW_offline.json -> FW_offline.json.gz, which causes FWS to not detect the offline file when recovering jobs.

Potential fixes
1. make it easier to turn off gzip and alert people in the docs this is necessary in order to use offline mode (not a preferred solution)
2. have FWS support FW_offline.json.gz so it doesn't matter if the file is gzipped or not
3. prevent / avoid having custodian gzip the FW_offline.json file in atomate.

Probably 2 is the best solution?

import httptools

import unittest
from unittest import mock

UPGRADE_RESPONSE = b'''HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols
UPGRADE: websocket

TRANSFER-ENCODING: chunked
CONNECTION: upgrade
DATE: Sat, 07 May 2016 23:44:32 GMT
SERVER: Python/3.4 aiohttp/1.0.3
\r\n\x89\x04data'''


class TestResponseParser(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_parser_upgrade_response(self):
        m = mock.Mock()

        headers = {}
        m.on_header.side_effect = headers.__setitem__

        p = httptools.HttpResponseParser(m)
        try:
            p.feed_data(UPGRADE_RESPONSE)
        except:
            pass

        self.assertEqual(p.get_http_version(), '1.1')
        self.assertEqual(p.get_status_code(), 101)

        m.on_status.assert_called_once_with(b'Switching Protocols')

        m.on_headers_complete.assert_called_once_with()
        self.assertEqual(m.on_header.call_count, 6)
        self.assertEqual(len(headers), 6)

        m.on_message_complete.assert_called_once_with()

```

issue_title: Using Meta classes for subclasses of graphene.ObjectType.
issue_body:
I am trying to make have a meta class for my graphene.ObjectType subclass. My main objective is to make attributes of class (i.e. fields of Graphene Object) from some list of strings that contain their names.

For eg. list = ['quantity', 'position']
For this list, my class Attack must something be like this :
```
class Attack(graphene.ObjectType):
    quantity = graphene.String()
    position = graphene.String()
```

The way I am doing this is as follows :
```
import graphene
from graphene.types.objecttype import ObjectType, ObjectTypeMeta

class mytype(ObjectTypeMeta):
    def __new__(cls, clsname, base, clsdict):
        print "Hello"
        setattr(cls, 'quantity', graphene.String())
        return ObjectTypeMeta.__new__(cls, clsname, base, clsdict)
        
class combined_meta(mytype, ObjectTypeMeta):
    pass

class Attack(graphene.ObjectType):
    __metaclass__ = combined_meta
    def __init__(self, dic):
        self.quantity = "123"
        print "Hello world"
        print dic

class Query(graphene.ObjectType):
    attack = graphene.Field(Attack)
    def resolve_attack(self, args, context, info):
        return Attack(dict())

schema = graphene.Schema(query = Query)

query = '''
{
    attack
}
'''

result = schema.execute(query)
print result['data']

```

I see ```"Hello"``` with whole lot many errors....
```
AssertionError: Attack fields must be a mapping (dict / OrderedDict) with field names as keys or a function which returns such a mapping.
```

Both the [`README`](../blob/master/README.md) and [the troubleshooting page](docs/troubleshooting.rst) contain this outdated advice:

> **Note:** The middleware placement is sensitive. If the middleware before `silk.middleware.SilkyMiddleware` returns from `process_request` then `SilkyMiddleware` will never get the chance to execute. Therefore you must ensure that any middleware placed before never returns anything from `process_request`. See the [django docs](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/topics/http/middleware/#process-request) for more information on this.

That "django docs" link hasn't gone to the right point in the Django middleware page since Django 1.9, which - as far as I can tell - is the last version that had `process_request()`. I eventually worked this out after having been scared by this note into trying to work out what it meant before placing the middleware.

I'd do a PR with improved advice, but I'm new enough to Silk that I don't know what it would be.

issue_title: Docker Builds fail when being executed in parallel
issue_body:
When 2 or more Docker Builds are being executed at the same time, the first one will run as expected, however the others will fail with the following error:
```
docker: Error response from daemon: Conflict. The container name "/conan_runner" is already in use by container "[...]". You have to remove (or rename) that container to be able to reuse that name.
See 'docker run --help'.
sha256:[...]
Error response from daemon: You cannot remove a running container [...]. Stop the container before attempting removal or force remove
```
A common situation where this behaviour could occur, would be when a CI-Server starts multiple Jobs in parallel (e.g. when building multiple libs with the same docker image).

From what I can tell, the error seems to be caused by the run-method from the DockerTestPackageRunner, which seems to update the docker image (I think?): https://github.com/conan-io/conan-package-tools/blob/a577cb65ae486c0f8330d2b1f09d9698e17eb9e3/conan/create_runner.py#L127
If this part is indeed the issue, would it be an acceptable solution to provide another environment variable (e.g. DOCKER_IMAGE_UPGRADE=[True,False]) in order to check whether or not to do the pull step? This would also be useful, when working with preconfigured/prebuilt docker images which use specific conan versions.

issue_title: cf sync hanging for s3 bucket policy
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm running cfn-sphere 0.1.35 on Mac OS X 10.11.6. I have the following single stack template that creates an S3 bucket and a policy for that bucket:

`
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: S3 bucket and policy to test hanging behaviour
Resources:
  hangS3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: '|ref|AWS::StackName'
  hangS3BucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: '|ref|hangS3Bucket'
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          -
            Action:
              - 's3:GetObject'
            Effect: 'Allow'
            Resource:
              Fn::Join:
                - ""
                -
                  - "arn:aws:s3:::"
                  - '|ref|hangS3Bucket'
                  - "/*"
            Principal: '*'`

When I run this with cf sync, the s3 bucket is created in about 30 seconds, but then the bucket policy hangs for 9 minutes 30 seconds until timing out.

If I modify the template slightly to be:

`
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: S3 bucket and policy to test hanging behaviour
Resources:
  hangS3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: '|ref|AWS::StackName'
  hangS3BucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: '|ref|hangS3Bucket'
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          -
            Action:
              - 's3:GetObject'
            Effect: 'Allow'
            Resource:
              Fn::Join:
                - ""
                -
                  - "arn:aws:s3:::"
                  -
                    Ref: "hangS3Bucket"
                  - "/*"
            Principal: '*'`

then there is no hanging at all, with the S3 bucket and the bucket policy being created in 37 seconds.

The only difference between these two templates is that the first uses '|ref|hangS3Bucket' within an Fn::Join statement inside the bucket policy document resource and the second uses Ref: "hangS3Bucket" instead.

Does using the '|ref|resource_id' style of referencing cause hanging behaviour within an Fn::Join statement? Running the first stack template does not give any errors about invalid template syntax, but merely runs for 10 minutes with no output after "hangS3BucketPolicy: CREATE_IN_PROGRESS:" and then times out.
Thread devices need to store active operational dataset and pending operational dataset on non-volatile memory properly, and could retrieve them after hardware reset.

issue_body:
During my workshop today, someone got a DialogError. It wasn't immediately clear what was wrong, but the message was pretty cryptic. We should give instructions to run with `--text` whenever a DialogError is thrown.

Alternately, maybe we should remove the dialog dependency entirely?

issue_title: Populate instructions in .rst files from instruction generator
issue_body: When we have an instruction generator (#2704) it will initially duplicate some strings from `using.rst` and `contributing.rst` in a JSON blob.  It looks theoretically possible to use [a mechanism like this](http://docutils.sourceforge.net/docs/howto/rst-roles.html) to load JSON elements into our reStructuredText to prevent string duplication.

issue_title: Add support to the Mystorm Blackice board
issue_body:
Main issue for giving support to the mystorm blackice open fpga board. This issue will be break down into smaller issues. This issue will be closed when the initial support is working ok

Roadmap
--------

* Add the blakice in the list of board, so that the build can be done with apio build command (and the .ini apio file)
* Create a python tool for downloading into the board (similar to cat > /dev/ttyUSB). This tool should be multiplatform (Windows, mac, linux). black-iceprog? (a name is needed)
* Include support for auto-detecting the blackice board serial port. I think this is the most difficult part
* Include the blackiceprog toolchain as part of apio (install, uninstall, etc...)
* Create a new scons file that calls blackiceprog instead of iceprog when the blackice board is used
* Implement the apio upload command for the blackice
* Include some examples: blackice/leds, etc...

issue_title: Unit-tests should only have a golden airflow spec in one place
issue_body: Currently this exists in kernel/bigquery_tests.py, kernel/pipeline_tests.py, and pipeline/pipeline_tests.py. Ideally, this should only be in kernel/bigquery_tests.py or kernel/pipeline_tests.py.

issue_body:
First of all, thanks a lot for this very useful library. I am building an LTI provider with Flask and I've been able to post back grades to the consumer without any difficulty!
However I am struggling to figure out how to validate the incoming launch request from the provider. Is this library supporting that? If yes, how should I proceed? 
I simply need to validate the Oauth signature and the various LTI params.
Let me know if you have any tips!

issue_title: Include flake8 check for trailing commas
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

No human being should be ever checking if there's a missing trailing comma in the code - we can offload this onto **A L G O R I T H M S**.

- [ ] add `flake8-commas` to `requirements.txt`
- [ ] run `make lint` to see where the commas are missing (*there's a lot of them*)
- [ ] add trailing commas to make `flake8` happy

#543 

There are four possible actions:

1. remove both file and signatures, trigger rerun
    * Thorough clean of files and signatures.
2. remove files but not signatures, trigger rerun
    * Can largely be achieved with `rm -rf`
3. keep files but remove signatures, trigger rerun
    * Clean `.sos`? There is no real need for this.
4. replace files with their signatures, does not trigger rerun
    * Remove large intermediate files.


### Proposal One

Only one command

```
sos remove FILE_OR_DIR
```

will replace files with their signatures.

Rational:
* Most easy to remember.
* Normal file-only removal can be done by `rm -f`
* Removal of signatures are not really needed.
* Removal of visible `.sig` files can trigger rerun

Cons:
* Can be counter-intuitive. 

### Proposal two

1. By default replace files by their signatures. This will NOT trigger rerun.
    `sos remove FILE_OR_DIR`
 
2. Remove signatures but not files (clean signatures only), this will trigger rerun.
    `sos remove FILE_OR_DIR -s`

3. Remove signatures and files (clean up everything), this will trigger rerun.
    `sos remove  FILE_OR_DIR -S`   (or `-a` for `all`)

### Proposal three

1. By default remove files and signatures. This will trigger rerun. Note that this is more consistent with what users expect, but again, `rm -f` has similar effect.
    `sos remove FILE_OR_DIR`
 
2. Remove signatures but not files (clean signatures only), for cleanup, will trigger rerun.
    `sos remove FILE_OR_DIR -s`

3. Replace files with their signatures, will not trigger rerun.
    `sos remove  FILE_OR_DIR -i`   (`-i` for `impersonate`, or `--replace`, or `-S`)

So default performs clean removal, `-i` is a special removal.

### Options

Limiting parameters to 
* Limit to tracked files (`-t`, `--tracked`)
    `sos remove  --tracked`
* Limit to untracked files (`-u`, `--untracked`)
    `sos remove  --untracked`
* Limit to files older or newer than age (`--age`)
    `sos remove --age +5d`
* Limit to files smaller or larger than size
   ```sos remove FILES --size +5G```

issue_title: Behavior of %with when calling from a non-SoS kernel.
issue_body:
I believe the current implementation assumes `SoS` as the current kernel so 

```
%with R -i var1 -o var2
```
sends `var` to `R` before switching and returns `var2` to `SoS` after completion of the cell.

Now, if we are in another kernel (e.g. `Matlab` as the default kernel), we should send `var1` in **Matlab** to `R` and return `var2` back to `Matlab`.

This is useful for applying transforms outside of a DataLoader (which does it on one image at a time).

issue_title: [rllib] A3C gradient magnitude is dependent on batch size
issue_body:
### Describe the problem

In https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/master/python/ray/rllib/agents/a3c/a3c_tf_policy_graph.py#L28 we use tf.reduce_sum() instead of reduce_mean, which makes the gradient magnitude dependent on batch size.

I also noticed that grad_gnorm is often hitting the clip value of 40.0.

@richardliaw what's the right fix here? Are the lr and grad clip value tuned for batch_size=20, in which case we should divide the by 20 and switch to reduce_mean?

RLlib should include a utility to search for reasonable hyper-parameters, e.g. via Hyperband: https://arxiv.org/abs/1603.06560 that implements early stopping of un-promising candidates.

Plugin error: Unable to parse XML: not well-formed (invalid token): line 1, column 762 (b"<?xml version='1.0'?><quality><pr ...)

Process finished with exit code 1
```

### Comments, screenshots, etc.

xml contains `&` characters, replacing with `&` gets it working.

Resolved by modifying streamlink\utils\__init__.py and adding `data = re.sub("&", "&", data.decode('utf8'))`


```
[plugin.rtve][debug] Found content with id: 4540424
OrderedDict([('540p_http', <HTTPStream('http://mvod.lvlt.rtve.es/resources/TE_NGVA/mp4/3/8/1522075587483.mp4')>), ('360p_http', <HTTPStream('http://mvod.lvlt.rtve.es/resources/TE_NGVA/mp4/1/1/1522075683411.mp4')>), ('270p_http', <HTTPStream('http://mvod.lvlt.rtve.es/resources/TE_NGVA/mp4/3/0/1522075727303.mp4')>), ('270p_alt', <HLSStream('http://hlsvod.lvlt.rtve.es/resources/TE_NGVA/mp4/3/0/1522075727303.mp4/1522075727303-audio=48001-video=620000.m3u8?hls_minimum_fragment_length=6&hls_client_manifest_version=3')>), ('270p', <HLSStream('http://hlsvod2017b.akamaized.net/resources/TE_NGVA/mp4/3/0/1522075727303.mp4/1522075727303-audio=48001-video=620000.m3u8?hls_minimum_fragment_length=6&hls_client_manifest_version=3')>), ('360p_alt', <HLSStream('http://hlsvod.lvlt.rtve.es/resources/TE_NGVA/mp4/1/1/1522075683411.mp4/1522075683411-audio=64001-video=720000.m3u8?hls_minimum_fragment_length=6&hls_client_manifest_version=3')>), ('360p', <HLSStream('http://hlsvod2017b.akamaized.net/resources/TE_NGVA/mp4/1/1/1522075683411.mp4/1522075683411-audio=64001-video=720000.m3u8?hls_minimum_fragment_length=6&hls_client_manifest_version=3')>), ('540p_alt', <HLSStream('http://hlsvod.lvlt.rtve.es/resources/TE_NGVA/mp4/3/8/1522075587483.mp4/1522075587483-audio=64001-video=1400000.m3u8?hls_minimum_fragment_length=6&hls_client_manifest_version=3')>), ('540p', <HLSStream('http://hlsvod2017b.akamaized.net/resources/TE_NGVA/mp4/3/8/1522075587483.mp4/1522075587483-audio=64001-video=1400000.m3u8?hls_minimum_fragment_length=6&hls_client_manifest_version=3')>), ('worst', <HLSStream('http://hlsvod.lvlt.rtve.es/resources/TE_NGVA/mp4/3/0/1522075727303.mp4/1522075727303-audio=48001-video=620000.m3u8?hls_minimum_fragment_length=6&hls_client_manifest_version=3')>), ('best', <HLSStream('http://hlsvod2017b.akamaized.net/resources/TE_NGVA/mp4/3/8/1522075587483.mp4/1522075587483-audio=64001-video=1400000.m3u8?hls_minimum_fragment_length=6&hls_client_manifest_version=3')>)])

Process finished with exit code 0

```


**Don't know if this breaks other plugins as I only use this one.**



```
def parse_xml(data, name="XML", ignore_ns=False, exception=PluginError, schema=None):
    """Wrapper around ElementTree.fromstring with some extras

    Provides these extra features:
     - Handles incorrectly encoded XML
     - Allows stripping namespace information
     - Wraps errors in custom exception with a snippet of the data in the message
    """
    if is_py2 and isinstance(data, unicode):
        data = data.encode("utf8")

    if ignore_ns:
        data = re.sub(" xmlns=\"(.+?)\"", "", data)

    data = re.sub("&", "&", data.decode('utf8'))

    try:
        tree = ET.fromstring(data)
    except Exception as err:
        snippet = repr(data)
        if len(snippet) > 35:
            snippet = snippet[:35] + " ..."

        raise exception("Unable to parse {0}: {1} ({2})".format(name, err, snippet))

    if schema:
        tree = schema.validate(tree, name=name, exception=exception)

    return tree

```

A more modest improvement would be to print out (formatted) just the command names, always.

usage: gitlab [-h] [--version] [-v] [-d] [-c CONFIG_FILE] [-g GITLAB]
              [-o {json,legacy,yaml}] [-f FIELDS] COMMAND

    ...
    user-project

To view options specific to a command, run 'gitlab COMMAND --help'

              [-o {json,legacy,yaml}] [-f FIELDS] COMMAND

issue_title: query generated in list for page > 1 adds an extra id parameter
issue_body:
## Description of the problem, including code/CLI snippet
When I query group membership with more than 1 page, the queries after the first one contain an extra id parameter which I believe is useless

```
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:https://:443 "GET /api/v4/groups/300/members?per_page=100&all=True HTTP/1.1" 200 27155
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:https://:443 "GET /api/v4/groups/300/members?all=True&id=300&page=2&per_page=100 HTTP/1.1" 200 26820
DEBUG:urllib3.connectionpool:https://:443 "GET /api/v4/groups/300/members?all=True&id=300&page=3&per_page=100 HTTP/1.1" 200 3218
```
=> `&id=300`

This id is the same as the group id.

## Expected Behavior
no extra id param

## Actual Behavior
extra id param

## Specifications

  - python-gitlab version: python-gitlab==1.4.0
  - API version you are using (v3/v4): v4
  - Gitlab server version (or gitlab.com): 9.4.5

## Description of the problem, including code/CLI snippet

example found on [Project members ](http://python-gitlab.readthedocs.io/en/master/gl_objects/projects.html#project-members)

code : 

```
gl = gitlab.Gitlab.from_config('project', ['/home/guest/.python-gitlab.cfg'])
gl.auth()

project = gl.projects.get(39)
members = project.members.list()
member = members[0]
print("member : %s" % member.username)
print("-------------------------------------")
member = project.members.get(1)
```
outputs : 

> member : trunk
> -------------------------------------
> Traceback (most recent call last):
>   File "./GetAllUsers.py", line 23, in <module>
>     member = project.members.get(1)
>   File "/home/buildbot/WorkSpace/abinit/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gitlab/exceptions.py", line 244, in wrapped_f
>     raise error(e.error_message, e.response_code, e.response_body)
> gitlab.exceptions.GitlabGetError: 404: 404 Not found

## Specifications

  - python-gitlab version:  1.5.1
  - API version you are using (v3/v4):  v4
  - Gitlab server version (or gitlab.com):  11.0.1

issue_title: 调度爬虫的时候可否增加 添加参数的功能？
issue_body:
平常调度爬虫的时候会 curl  ..... -a arg1=value1  -a arg2=value2  -a arg3=value3 传参数，在使用 Gerapy的时候，调度爬虫，点run后就直接提交运行了。

这个虽然可以直接修改文件里面的参数来做这样的操作，但是还是要修改文件代码，总觉得有点麻烦。

要是能直接在调度的时候添加的话就挺好的。

```
> Error
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/unittest/case.py", line 329, in run

  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/test/cwl/cwlTest.py", line 58, in _debug_worker_tester
    os.path.join(rootDir, jobfile)], stdout=st)
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/cwl/cwltoil.py", line 1065, in main
    outobj = toil.start(wf1)
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/common.py", line 783, in start
    return self._runMainLoop(rootJobGraph)
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/common.py", line 1057, in _runMainLoop
    jobCache=self._jobCache).run()
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/leader.py", line 212, in run
    self.innerLoop()
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/leader.py", line 521, in innerLoop
    self._processReadyJobs()
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/leader.py", line 445, in _processReadyJobs
    self._processReadyJob(jobGraph, resultStatus)
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/leader.py", line 415, in _processReadyJob
    self._runJobSuccessors(jobGraph)
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/leader.py", line 333, in _runJobSuccessors
    self.issueJobs(successors)
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/leader.py", line 614, in issueJobs
    self.issueJob(job)
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/leader.py", line 593, in issueJob
    jobBatchSystemID = self.batchSystem.issueBatchJob(jobNode)
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/batchSystems/singleMachine.py", line 246, in issueBatchJob
    self.worker(self.inputQueue)
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/batchSystems/singleMachine.py", line 203, in worker
    self._runWorker(jobCommand, jobID, environment)
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/batchSystems/singleMachine.py", line 156, in _runWorker
    redirectOutputToLogFile=not self.debugWorker) # Call the worker
  File "/home/ben/git/updateDependencies/toil/src/toil/worker.py", line 457, in workerScript
    with open(tempWorkerLogPath, "r") as f:

You can only write this file (`tempWorkerLogPath`) when `redirectOutputToLogFile` is true. However, the file is attempted to be read only after checking that `workerFailed` is true.

This can be turned off:
https://github.com/DataBiosphere/toil/blob/master/src/toil/worker.py#L186

But it always checks here:
https://github.com/DataBiosphere/toil/blob/master/src/toil/worker.py#L457

issue_title: Better docstring format for API Documentation (optional)
issue_body:
Stylistically, it may be better to pattern the docstring with the standard template found [here](https://pythonhosted.org/an_example_pypi_project/sphinx.html#function-definitions). This is so that it will render properly in your [API Documentation](http://niapy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/algorithms.html#module-NiaPy.algorithms.basic).

Another option is to use the [`numpydoc` extension](https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc), which gives a prettier format inside your docstrings. 

--- 
This is an issue related to openjournals/joss-reviews#613
